reasoning: Given the empirical base rate of 0.225 for any-increase ACLED questions, I begin near this value. ACLED fatality distributions are heavily right-skewed (driven by infrequent, high-fatality spikes), meaning the temporal mean (31.167) is significantly higher than the median. As a result, any randomly selected 30-day period has a lower-than-50% chance of exceeding the 12-month mean. The first window (Oct 3 - Nov 2) is almost entirely before the cutoff and likely represents a standard month, justifying a probability close to the base rate (~0.24). As the forecast horizon extends to Nov 25 and Jan 24, mechanical uncertainty pulls the probabilities towards 0.5. Furthermore, Bangladesh's typical election season falls around late December/January, which combined with the extended transition timeline of the interim government installed in August 2024, introduces a tangible risk of election-related violence spiking early in 2026. Therefore, I incrementally raise the probability for November away from the base rate, and more significantly for January up to 0.35.
